@@ -43,7 +43,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-32g","title":"[ubu.9] Handle LTS-to-LTS upgrade path special case","description":"# Handle LTS-to-LTS Upgrade Path Special Case ## Purpose Ubuntu LTS releases can upgrade directly to the next LTS (22.04 → 24.04) OR follow the regular path through intermediate releases. We need to handle this correctly. ## Background Ubuntu upgrade paths have special rules: - **Normal releases**: Must upgrade sequentially (24.10 → 25.04 → 25.10) - **LTS releases**: Can upgrade directly to next LTS (22.04 → 24.04) - **LTS to normal**: Requires enabling "Prompt=normal" in /etc/update-manager/release-upgrades ## Default Behavior By default, do-release-upgrade on LTS systems only shows LTS upgrades. To see non-LTS upgrades, need to modify configuration: ```bash # In /etc/update-manager/release-upgrades # Change: Prompt=lts # To: Prompt=normal ``` ## Implementation ### Detection ```bash # Check if current system is LTS ubuntu_is_lts() { local version=$(ubuntu_get_version_string) # LTS versions: 22.04, 24.04, 26.04, etc. [[ "$version" =~ ^[0-9]+\.04$ ]] } ``` ### Path Calculation for LTS Systems ```bash ubuntu_calculate_upgrade_path() { local target="$1" local current=$(ubuntu_get_version_string) if ubuntu_is_lts; then # Check if we should go LTS-to-LTS or through normal releases local next_lts=$(ubuntu_get_next_lts "$current") if ubuntu_version_gte "$next_lts" "$target"; then # Target is the next LTS or earlier - go direct echo "$next_lts" else # Target is beyond next LTS - need to go through normal releases # Enable normal release channel ubuntu_enable_normal_releases ubuntu_calculate_sequential_path "$current" "$target" fi else ubuntu_calculate_sequential_path "$current" "$target" fi } ``` ### Enable Normal Releases ```bash ubuntu_enable_normal_releases() { local config="/etc/update-manager/release-upgrades" if [[ -f "$config" ]]; then # Backup original cp "$config" "${config}.acfs-backup" # Change Prompt=lts to Prompt=normal sed -i "s/^Prompt=lts$/Prompt=normal/" "$config" log_info "Enabled normal release upgrades (was LTS-only)" fi } ``` ## Example Upgrade Paths ### From 22.04 LTS to 25.10 Option A (LTS path first): - 22.04 → 24.04 (LTS hop) - 24.04 → 24.10 → 25.04 → 25.10 (normal path) Option B (all normal): - 22.04 → 22.10 → 23.04 → 23.10 → 24.04 → 24.10 → 25.04 → 25.10 **We choose Option A** - fewer hops, more stable. ### From 24.04 LTS to 25.10 - 24.04 → 24.10 → 25.04 → 25.10 (normal path, 3 hops) ## Configuration Restoration After reaching target version, restore original LTS-only setting: ```bash ubuntu_restore_lts_only() { local config="/etc/update-manager/release-upgrades" local backup="${config}.acfs-backup" if [[ -f "$backup" ]]; then mv "$backup" "$config" log_info "Restored LTS-only release setting" fi } ``` ## Testing Notes - Test from 22.04: Verify LTS-to-LTS hop works - Test from 24.04: Verify normal path enables correctly - Test config restoration after completion","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:24:05.237039-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:39:24.893274-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:39:24.893274-05:00","close_reason":"Added LTS detection (ubuntu_is_lts - even years only), next LTS calculation (ubuntu_get_next_lts), and release channel management (ubuntu_enable_normal_releases, ubuntu_restore_lts_only).","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-32g","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9xt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:24:40.623353-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-33ug","title":"error_tracking: harden get_error_context_json","description":"scripts/lib/error_tracking.sh was emitting invalid/unsafe JSON in the no-jq fallback (insufficient escaping, empty fields rendered as string 'null') and could break jq mode if LAST_ERROR_CODE is non-numeric (jq --argjson). Harden JSON building to properly escape and emit nulls, and normalize exit_code to a numeric value.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T21:11:59.930522-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T21:13:55.507176-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T21:13:55.507176-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in mainline commit a5565e3 (fix(error_tracking): correct JSON fallback encoding)."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-367d","title":"Audit and expand Jargon component terms","description":"# Task: Audit and Expand Jargon Component Terms ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Jargon System & Documentation Polish (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-7i45) ## Description The Jargon component provides tooltip definitions for technical terms, but coverage is incomplete and some definitions are too technical. Need a comprehensive audit. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/components/jargon.tsx ### Terms to audit/add: - VPS - Virtual Private Server (already exists, verify quality) - SSH - Secure Shell (verify quality) - Terminal - text interface (verify quality) - Command line - same as terminal (alias?) - Root - superuser/admin account - sudo - run as superuser - Ubuntu user - regular user account - tmux - terminal multiplexer - Session - persistent terminal environment - Repository/Repo - code storage folder - Git - version control system - API key - authentication token - Environment variable - system setting - Port - network endpoint - IP address - network address - zsh - shell program - Bash - shell program ### Quality criteria for definitions: - No jargon in the definition itself - One sentence maximum for tooltip - Relatable analogy if possible - Consistent tone ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All terms listed above have definitions - [ ] Definitions are beginner-friendly (no technical jargon) - [ ] Tooltip text fits comfortably (not too long) - [ ] All wizard pages use Jargon wrappers appropriately - [ ] Mobile tooltip experience is good (tap to show) ## UI/UX Notes - Tooltips should be quick reads (2-3 seconds) - Consider linking to glossary for longer explanations - Test on mobile — touch targets and positioning","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:44:46.425151-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:34:55.414351-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:34:55.414351-05:00","close_reason":"Added 4 new beginner-friendly jargon terms: session, api-key, environment-variable, clone. All terms include short/long descriptions, analogies, and 'why we use it' explanations. Checked for and avoided duplicates with existing terms."} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-37rw","title":"EPIC: Learning Hub World-Class UI/UX Overhaul","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-25T00:12:33.534668-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T00:12:33.534668-05:00"} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-37rw","title":"EPIC: Learning Hub World-Class UI/UX Overhaul","description":"Transform the Learning Hub section of the ACFS website from its current functional-but-basic state to match the world-class, Stripe-quality UI/UX polish of the main landing page. This includes: **Context & Motivation:** - The main landing page has beautiful animations, gradients, hover states, and cohesive design - The Learning Hub pages look noticeably less polished in comparison ('absolute shit' per user feedback) - Users navigating from the slick landing page to the Learning Hub experience jarring quality drop - This harms user confidence and overall perception of the project **Goals:** 1. Visual consistency with landing page design language 2. World-class desktop experience (sidebars, multi-column, keyboard nav) 3. World-class mobile experience (touch-friendly, gestures, thumb-zone) 4. Framer Motion animations matching landing page quality 5. Progress celebration, gamification elements 6. Polished micro-interactions on every interactive element **Pages in scope:** - /learn (dashboard) - /learn/[slug] (lessons) - /learn/glossary - /learn/commands - /learn/agent-commands - /learn/ntm-palette - /learn/tools/[tool] **Success Criteria:** Users should 'gasp at how stunning and perfect it is in every way'","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-25T00:12:33.534668-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T00:12:52.168141-05:00"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-37sa","title":"Add p10k wizard handling instructions","description":"# Task: Add Powerlevel10k Wizard Handling ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Post-Install Transition Experience (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r2np) ## Description On first zsh login, Powerlevel10k may launch a configuration wizard. This is unexpected and confusing for beginners. They don't know what it's asking or if they should configure it. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/reconnect-ubuntu/page.tsx Add a GuideTip or AlertCard: \`\`\`tsx Prompt customization wizard? When you first connect, you might see a "Powerlevel10k configuration wizard" asking about fonts and prompt style. You can:
  • Press q to quit and use defaults (recommended for now)
  • Or go through it if you want to customize your prompt appearance

You can always run p10k configure later to customize.

\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Warns about possible p10k wizard - [ ] Clear instruction to press 'q' to skip - [ ] Mentions ability to configure later - [ ] Non-scary tone (it's a feature, not a problem) ## UI/UX Notes - Use GuideTip (not warning) — this is informational - Keep it brief; most users will just press q - "Recommended for now" guides beginners to the simpler path","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:43:03.190685-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:35:18.797985-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:35:18.797985-05:00","close_reason":"Added a friendly tip about the p10k wizard (press q to skip; p10k configure later)"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3ci","title":"Fix acfs doctor --json output (pure JSON + correct counts)","description":"scripts/lib/doctor.sh --json currently prints section headings before JSON, doesn’t increment pass/warn/fail counters in JSON mode, and doesn’t escape strings. Make --json emit only valid JSON on stdout with correct summary counts (and handle missing /etc/os-release).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2025-12-20T13:44:19.646755-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T13:49:30.854595-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T13:49:30.854595-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed scripts/lib/doctor.sh --json to emit pure valid JSON (no headings), increment counters in JSON mode, and JSON-escape strings; also handles missing /etc/os-release."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3dkg","title":"TASK: Implement info.sh core data gathering","description":"# TASK: Implement info.sh core data gathering ## Context Implement the core data gathering functions for `acfs info`. These read from state files and system commands to populate the info data model. ## Implementation ### File Location `scripts/lib/info.sh` ### Functions to Implement ```bash # Get system hostname info_get_hostname() { cat /etc/hostname 2>/dev/null || hostname } # Get primary IP address info_get_ip() { hostname -I 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}' || echo "unknown" } # Get human-readable uptime info_get_uptime() { uptime -p 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^up //' || echo "unknown" } # Get OS version info info_get_os() { if [[ -f /etc/os-release ]]; then source /etc/os-release echo "${VERSION_ID:-unknown} (${VERSION_CODENAME:-unknown})" else echo "unknown" fi } # Get installation state from state.json info_get_install_state() { local state_file="${ACFS_HOME:-$HOME/.acfs}/state.json" if [[ -f "$state_file" ]]; then # Parse with jq if available, fallback to grep ... fi } # Get onboard progress info_get_onboard_progress() { local progress_file="${ACFS_HOME:-$HOME/.acfs}/onboard_progress.json" ... } ``` ### Error Handling - All functions should have fallback values - Never fail/exit on missing data - Return "unknown" or empty for missing info ### Performance Requirements - Each function should complete in <100ms - No network calls - No spawning heavy processes ## Testing Create test cases: ```bash # Test with missing files rm -f ~/.acfs/state.json info_get_install_state # Should return defaults # Test with valid files # ... ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All data gathering functions implemented - [ ] Fallback values for missing data - [ ] No network calls - [ ] Each function <100ms - [ ] Works when state files missing","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:56:27.287782-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:07:45.632495-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:07:45.632495-05:00","close_reason":"Complete implementation: info.sh with terminal, JSON, HTML, minimal output formats; integrated into acfs CLI","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3dkg","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-cxz3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:56:44.743105-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
